summary: Semi-finalists in 2022 — the first Arab and African side to reach the last four — now under new coach Mohamed Ouahbi, who replaced Walid Regragui in March (he led Morocco's U-20s to their first World Cup title in 2024). Only four games in, but the wide-combination identity and defensive compactness carry over.
style: Attacking, wide-combination football (evolved from Regragui's 4-3-3) — full-backs, midfielders and wingers combining on the flanks, with overlapping runs from Hakimi a key weapon. Defensive organisation and compactness without the ball remain a cornerstone. Capable of a high press from a 4-2-3-1.

counterReliance.note: Attacking, possession-and-crossing led on a compact defensive base

strengths: Excellent defensive organisation and compactness (the foundation of the 2022 semi-final run)
Wide combinations and overlapping full-backs, headlined by Hakimi
Aggressive, coordinated high press when they choose to engage
Real attacking quality through the half-spaces
weaknesses: Small sample under Ouahbi (4 games) — system still settling close to the tournament

keyPlayers.name: Brahim Díaz
keyPlayers.note: Player of the 2025 AFCON (5 goals, creativity); under Ouahbi plays a narrower right-half-space role suiting his profile

keyPlayers.name: Achraf Hakimi
keyPlayers.note: The overlapping width and end product on the right — central to the wide combinations

keyPlayers.name: Bilal El Khannouss
keyPlayers.note: Pacey, direct creator — the run into the box that finishes the long build-up moves

tactics: Build through wide combinations and overlapping full-backs on a compact, organised defensive base; press high when the moment suits. Whether they press Brazil in the opener (MetLife, June 13) is a key tactical question. A continent will hope they relive 2022.
notes: Won the 2025 AFCON on home soil (awarded after a contested 1-0 final loss to Senegal, now under CAS appeal).
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group C (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
